title: Citrusy Gooey Vietnamese Myeongnan-roe Bowl
description: A crowd-pleasing, vegetarian-friendly Vietnamese dish featuring myeongnan-roe, deep-fried tofu, and a tangy citrus sauce. This dish is perfect for a weeknight dinner or a special occasion.
instructions: Heat 1 inch of vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut the tofu into 1 inch cubes and deep-fry until golden brown.
Drain the fried tofu on paper towels.
In a small saucepan, combine the grated ginger, garlic, fish-free sweet soy sauce, grated lime zest, fresh lime juice, sugar, patis, and chopped green onions.
Bring the sauce to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the cornstarch and water until smooth.
Add the cornstarch mixture to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Bring the sauce to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 minute.
Add the fried tofu to the saucepan and toss to coat with the citrus sauce.
Season with salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper to taste.
Serve the Citrusy Gooey Vietnamese Myeongnan-roe Bowl hot, garnished with additional green onions and myeongnan-roe sauce if desired.
